target.title: fix(state): refuse chmod-less agent database volumes that cannot prove credential privacy
target.body:
## Summary

The per-agent SQLite database stores credentials (API keys and OAuth tokens under `agents/<agentId>/agent/`). On filesystems that reject `chmod` (Azure Files, NFS without `no_root_squash`, some Docker/SMB volume drivers), `ensureOpenClawAgentDatabasePermissions` called raw `chmodSync` and the agent could not open its database at all: it threw an uncaught `ENOTSUP`/`EPERM` before the handle opened, while the non-secret shared state DB came up fine.

This is the follow-up to #92786, which closed that gap by reusing the shared state DB helper `bestEffortChmodSync`. @vincentkoc closed #92786 because that helper is too permissive for a secret store:

> The POSIX repair can fail closed while tolerating chmod-less volumes only when `stat` proves the target already private, but native Windows does not expose equivalent ACL guarantees through POSIX mode bits. Skipping the check makes permissive ACLs possible; enforcing it breaks supported Windows opens. ... A canonical fix needs an ACL-aware Windows implementation/verification plus platform-specific proof, or a clearly scoped policy that refuses unsafe Windows/network-backed agent DB locations. The shared non-secret state DB can retain its existing best-effort behavior; the agent DB cannot.

This PR implements the second canonical path: a scoped, fail-closed policy that refuses unsafe locations for the secret agent DB. The shared non-secret state DB is untouched and keeps its best-effort behavior.

## Fix

New `enforcePrivateAgentDbChmodSync` used for the agent DB directory and sidecar files, replacing the raw `chmodSync` calls. Policy when `chmod` fails:

- Windows (`process.platform === "win32"`): refuse. Windows file modes do not reflect NTFS ACLs, so a failed `chmod` on a network-backed/SMB volume gives no proof the credential store is private. Throw a scoped, actionable error pointing the operator at a local path via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
- POSIX: tolerate only when `stat` proves the target is already private (`mode & 0o077 === 0`); the secret is protected and the `chmod` was cosmetic, so warn once and continue. Otherwise refuse with the same scoped error rather than leave credentials group or world reachable.

This is deliberately stricter than the sibling `bestEffortChmodSync`, which tolerates `ENOTSUP`/`EOPNOTSUPP`/`EINVAL` unconditionally for the non-secret store. There is intentionally no `EPERM` filesystem probe here: for a credential store the only safe question is whether the secret is private now, not whether the volume rejects `chmod`. `EACCES` and ordinary non-private `EPERM` faults fall into the refuse branch and stay loud.

The post-open permission pass now runs inside the same cleanup `catch` as schema setup. The refusal can fire on the second (post-open) pass when the agent directory is private but the freshly created DB file is born group/world readable on a chmod-less mount; previously that pass sat outside the cleanup, so a refusal left the live SQLite handle and WAL maintenance open. The handle and WAL helper are now closed before the error escapes.

Key behavior contrast with the closed PR: #92786 would silently open a world-readable secret DB on a chmod-less volume. This PR refuses that case while still opening when the store is provably private.

## Verification

- Colocated regression suite `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.permissions.test.ts` (6 cases): chmod success hardens to `0700`/`0600`; chmod fails but store already private opens; chmod fails on a non-private dir refuses; `win32` chmod failure refuses; `EACCES` on a non-private dir refuses; and a post-open refusal closes the freshly opened handle (asserts the captured `DatabaseSync` instance reports `isOpen === false`). Run with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/openclaw-agent-db.permissions.test.ts` -> 6 passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts` -> 12 passed (success path unchanged).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/openclaw-state-db.permissions.test.ts` -> 7 passed (non-secret best-effort path unchanged).
- `oxlint` on the changed files -> clean.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opening the per-agent credential database on a volume that rejects chmod now opens only when the store is provably private, and otherwise refuses with a scoped error instead of either crashing (old behavior) or silently storing credentials with permissive modes. A refusal that fires after the database handle is already open now closes that handle and its WAL maintenance instead of leaking them.

Real environment tested: Linux (Node 22.19) running the production entry points `openOpenClawAgentDatabase` and the auth-profile reader `readPersistedAuthProfileStoreRaw` against a real temporary state dir. The chmod-rejecting volume is reproduced at the OS layer: an LD_PRELOAD shim makes libc chmod(2)/fchmod(2)/fchmodat(2) return ENOTSUP or EACCES, so Node receives the real errno from the kernel call boundary. Store privacy is varied with the process umask (077 produces a private 0700/0600 store; 000 produces a group/world readable 0644 DB file, which only becomes visible to the second permission pass after open). Open file descriptors are counted from `/proc/self/fd` after a refusal to show whether the SQLite handle leaked.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `gcc -shared -fPIC -o chmodfail.so chmodfail.c`; then for each scenario `( umask <077|000>; LD_PRELOAD=$PWD/chmodfail.so CHMOD_FAIL_CODE=<ENOTSUP|EACCES> node_modules/.bin/tsx drive-agent-db.mts )` where the driver calls the real `openOpenClawAgentDatabase`, then `readPersistedAuthProfileStoreRaw` on the returned handle, and on a thrown refusal scans `/proc/self/fd` for any descriptor still pointing at `openclaw-agent.sqlite`. The before captures revert the single source file to `origin/main` and to this PR's pre-cleanup head; the same shim is used for before and after.

Evidence after fix: redacted live terminal output (non-secret `state/db` best-effort warn lines trimmed).

```
# BEFORE (origin/main, raw chmodSync), ENOTSUP volume, private store (umask 077) -> crashes:
$ ( umask 077; LD_PRELOAD=$PWD/chmodfail.so CHMOD_FAIL_CODE=ENOTSUP node_modules/.bin/tsx drive-agent-db.mts )
agent DB open: REFUSED - ENOTSUP: operation not supported on socket, chmod '<STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent'
OPEN_DB_FDS=0
REAL_EXIT=1

# AFTER (this PR), ENOTSUP volume, private store (umask 077) -> opens:
$ ( umask 077; LD_PRELOAD=$PWD/chmodfail.so CHMOD_FAIL_CODE=ENOTSUP node_modules/.bin/tsx drive-agent-db.mts )
agent DB open: db.isOpen=true path=<STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite
auth profile store load: OK (store=null)
REAL_EXIT=0

# BEFORE (this PR's pre-cleanup head 77b09fb), ENOTSUP volume, group/world readable DB file (umask 000)
#   -> post-open pass refuses but LEAKS the open handle and WAL sidecars:
$ ( umask 000; LD_PRELOAD=$PWD/chmodfail.so CHMOD_FAIL_CODE=ENOTSUP node_modules/.bin/tsx drive-agent-db.mts )
agent DB open: REFUSED - OpenClaw agent database <STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite stores credentials and cannot be made private on this volume (Error: ENOTSUP: operation not supported on socket, ...). Move the agent database to a filesystem that enforces private permissions by setting OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR to a local directory.
OPEN_DB_FDS=3
  still open: <STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite
  still open: <STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite-wal
  still open: <STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite-shm
REAL_EXIT=1

# AFTER (this PR), ENOTSUP volume, group/world readable DB file (umask 000)
#   -> post-open pass refuses AND closes the handle (no leaked descriptors):
$ ( umask 000; LD_PRELOAD=$PWD/chmodfail.so CHMOD_FAIL_CODE=ENOTSUP node_modules/.bin/tsx drive-agent-db.mts )
agent DB open: REFUSED - OpenClaw agent database <STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite stores credentials and cannot be made private on this volume (Error: ENOTSUP: operation not supported on socket, ...). Move the agent database to a filesystem that enforces private permissions by setting OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR to a local directory.
OPEN_DB_FDS=0
REAL_EXIT=1

# AFTER (this PR), EACCES volume, group/world readable DB file (umask 000) -> refuses (stays loud), handle closed:
$ ( umask 000; LD_PRELOAD=$PWD/chmodfail.so CHMOD_FAIL_CODE=EACCES node_modules/.bin/tsx drive-agent-db.mts )
agent DB open: REFUSED - OpenClaw agent database <STATE_DIR>/agents/main/agent/openclaw-agent.sqlite stores credentials and cannot be made private on this volume (Error: EACCES: permission denied, ...). Move the agent database to a filesystem that enforces private permissions by setting OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR to a local directory.
OPEN_DB_FDS=0
REAL_EXIT=1
```

Observed result after fix: On a chmod-less volume the agent DB opens (exit 0) only when the credential store is provably private; when the store would be group or world readable it refuses with a scoped, actionable error (exit 1) instead of crashing opaquely or storing credentials permissively. When that refusal fires after the handle is already open, the descriptor count to `openclaw-agent.sqlite` drops from 3 leaked descriptors on the pre-cleanup head to 0 on this PR, so the SQLite handle and WAL maintenance are released. A genuine EACCES fault stays loud. The unpatched `origin/main` crashed even on the private store (exit 1).

What was not tested: A literal Azure Files / NFS / Docker volume or a native Windows SMB mount end to end. The chmod rejection is reproduced at the libc syscall boundary via LD_PRELOAD, and the Windows refusal path is exercised by overriding `process.platform` to `win32` in the regression suite; real NTFS ACL semantics were not exercised on Linux. A future approach with real Windows ACL verification could relax the Windows refusal.

Refs: #92786 (closed; this is its canonical follow-up), #91919 (the shared state DB best-effort fix this intentionally does not reuse for the secret store).

CC @vincentkoc for the canonical-fix review.
target.author: yetval
